title: A Re-Assurance
content:
With what doubting eyes, oh sparrow,
    Thou regardest me,
    Underneath yon spray of yarrow,
    Dipping cautiously.

    Fear me not, oh little sparrow,
    Bathe and never fear,
    For to me both pool and yarrow
    And thyself are dear.
meaning:
"A Re-Assurance" is a quintessential example of Archibald Lampman's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
